title: Warn on BoltDB usage
body:
We started logging this in 5.6. In 5.7, we up to a warning. The upcoming 5.8 will up the warnings further to errors.

Required as we're removing BoltDB support in 6.0 next Spring.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Podman now warns when started with a BoltDB database. Support for BoltDB will be removed in Podman 6.0.
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

The boltdb tests failing are unsurprising, need to add the environment variable to suppress the warning. Or, alternatively, make it respect CI_DESIRED_DATABASE as well? The sqlite failures are not expected. Looks like `podman search` is looking for an image that no longer exists?

LGTM
once we get the tests happy.
fwiw, i agree with @Luap99 about going to WARN and that's it.

@Luap99 The 4.3.1 upgrade test is, I think, an expected break. It's pre-SQLite default, so I don't think we expect that upgrade test to succeed anymore. Agree with removing, or should we wait until I tear out Bolt entirely next week?

The test must stay since we still support it until it gets removed, would be nice to know if break upgrades in 5.8 for example, etc... Just do `export SUPPRESS_BOLTDB_WARNING=true` at the top somewhere in the test file I would say.

We can remove that old version from the upgrade tests once boltdb is actually removed, and in general it seem upgrade tests are neglected. There is no 5.x upgrade from test at all that is run so I doubt we cover much here.
CI seems stuck - jobs scheduled, but not started, for 90 minutes

Tests still failing you need this most likely (untested)

```diff
$ git diff
diff --git a/test/system/252-quadlet.bats b/test/system/252-quadlet.bats
index 0e9e9d9b83..901295db9e 100644
--- a/test/system/252-quadlet.bats
+++ b/test/system/252-quadlet.bats
@@ -207,6 +207,8 @@ function wait_for_journal() {
     # Ref https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21563#issuecomment-1965145324
     local quadlet_file=$PODMAN_TMPDIR/basic_$(safename).container
     cat > $quadlet_file <<EOF
+[Service]
+Environment=SUPPRESS_BOLTDB_WARNING=true
 [Container]
 Image=$IMAGE
 # Note it is important that the trap is before the ready message,
```
